prompt: The test always passes even when the method has a bug. What is wrong?
choices: JUnit automatically validates method return values
The test has no assertion; it prints the result but never verifies it, so it always passes regardless of what add() returns
System.out.println() counts as an assertion in JUnit 5
The test must call add() with at least three arguments
explanation: A test without assertions is a "passing test that tests nothing." It will pass whether add() returns 8, 2, or throws an exception (in some cases). Fix: add assertEquals(8, add(5, 3)); to verify the actual result against the expected value.
